cmd/jobs/billing/advance.purpose: Cobra sub-command package for invoice advance operations (list, advance single invoice, advance all). All service access flows exclusively through the `internal.App.BillingAutoAdvancer` singleton wired by app/common; no local service construction is permitted.

patterns.name: internal.App singleton access
patterns.description: All service calls go through `internal.App.<Field>`, never construct services locally. The singleton is populated by Wire before Cobra executes.

patterns.name: cmd.Context() for context propagation
patterns.description: Always pass `cmd.Context()` to service calls — never `context.Background()` — so cancellation and deadlines from Cobra's context chain are respected.

patterns.name: Sub-command factory functions
patterns.description: Each sub-command is a `var <Name>Cmd = func() *cobra.Command { ... }` factory, not a top-level var. Allows deferred flag registration and avoids init-order issues.
patterns.example: var ListCmd = func() *cobra.Command { cmd := &cobra.Command{Use: "list", ...}; return cmd }

patterns.name: PersistentFlags on parent Cmd
patterns.description: Flags shared across sub-commands (e.g. `--namespace`) are registered on the parent `Cmd` via `Cmd.PersistentFlags()` in `init()`, not on individual sub-commands.

patterns.name: Nil slice for optional namespace filter
patterns.description: Namespace filter is a `[]string`; build it conditionally so the service receives nil (unfiltered) when no namespace is specified, not an empty slice.
patterns.example: var ns []string; if namespace != "" { ns = append(ns, namespace) }

key_file_guides.role: Defines parent Cmd plus ListCmd, InvoiceCmd, AllCmd factory functions for invoice advance operations.
key_file_guides.watch_for: InvoiceCmd validates namespace is non-empty before calling AdvanceInvoice — replicate this guard on any new sub-command that requires a scoped namespace; omitting it produces a runtime error with no stack trace.

anti_patterns: Constructing billing services locally instead of using internal.App
Registering sub-command-specific flags on the parent Cmd (causes flag pollution across siblings)
Hardcoding namespace strings instead of accepting them via --namespace flag
Calling AdvanceInvoice without first validating that namespace is non-empty

decisions.decision: Thin Cobra wrappers delegating to internal.App service fields
decisions.rationale: Keeps cmd/* free of business logic; all wiring happens in app/common via Wire so each sub-command is independently testable and the DI graph is compile-time verified.

code_examples.scenario: Adding a new advance sub-command that advances invoices for a specific customer

cmd/jobs/billing/advancecharges.purpose: Cobra sub-command package for charge advance operations (list customers with pending charges, advance a single customer, advance all). Guards every execution path against `internal.App.ChargesAutoAdvancer == nil` because charges are an optional, conditionally-wired feature.

patterns.name: Nil guard for optional features
patterns.description: Check `internal.App.ChargesAutoAdvancer == nil` at the start of every RunE; return a descriptive error instead of allowing a nil pointer panic. The field is nil when charges are disabled in config.
patterns.example: if internal.App.ChargesAutoAdvancer == nil { return fmt.Errorf("charges are not enabled") }

patterns.name: internal.App singleton access
patterns.description: All service calls go through `internal.App.<Field>`; never construct adapters locally.

patterns.name: cmd.Context() for context propagation
patterns.description: Pass `cmd.Context()` to every service call; never substitute `context.Background()`.

patterns.name: Sub-command factory functions
patterns.description: Each sub-command is a `var <Name>Cmd = func() *cobra.Command` factory registered in `init()` via `Cmd.AddCommand()`.
patterns.example: var AllCmd = func() *cobra.Command { cmd := &cobra.Command{Use: "all", ...}; return cmd }

patterns.name: PersistentFlags on parent Cmd for shared filters
patterns.description: --namespace is registered on the parent Cmd so all sub-commands inherit it without re-registration.

key_file_guides.role: Defines parent Cmd plus ListCmd, CustomerCmd, AllCmd for charge advancement. CustomerCmd requires --namespace and exactly one CUSTOMER_ID arg.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Every RunE must check `ChargesAutoAdvancer != nil` first — the field is nil when billing charges are disabled in config. Missing this guard causes a panic with no user-friendly message.

anti_patterns: Omitting the nil check on ChargesAutoAdvancer — causes panic when charges feature is disabled
Constructing charges services locally instead of using internal.App
Registering --namespace as a persistent flag on a sub-command instead of the parent Cmd

decisions.decision: Explicit nil check for optional ChargesAutoAdvancer field at every RunE entry
decisions.rationale: Charges are a conditionally-wired feature; Wire produces a nil pointer when disabled. A nil guard yields a clear user-facing error instead of a runtime panic, keeping CLI UX consistent with other optional features.

code_examples.scenario: Adding a new charge sub-command that requires the optional ChargesAutoAdvancer

cmd/jobs/billing/collect.purpose: Cobra sub-command package for invoice collection operations (list collectable gathering invoices, collect for a specific customer, collect all). Uses structured input types from `billingworkercollect` rather than positional primitives to keep the CLI compatible with service API evolution.

patterns.name: Structured input types from domain packages
patterns.description: Pass `billingworkercollect.ListCollectableInvoicesInput` and `CollectCustomerInvoiceInput` structs rather than inline primitives; the compiler enforces required fields and the CLI stays compatible when the service signature evolves.

patterns.name: time.Now() as default AsOf/CollectionAt
patterns.description: Collection operations default to `time.Now()` for the temporal boundary. Expose a flag only if callers need to override.

patterns.name: StringSliceVar for multi-value filters
patterns.description: Multi-value filters (namespaces, customerIDs, invoiceIDs) use `cmd.PersistentFlags().StringSliceVar`, accepting comma-separated or repeated flag values.

patterns.name: cmd.Context() for context propagation
patterns.description: Always pass `cmd.Context()` to service calls; never substitute `context.Background()`.

patterns.name: Sub-command factory functions
patterns.description: Each sub-command is a factory var registered in `init()` via `Cmd.AddCommand()`; flags are registered inside the factory body.
patterns.example: var AllCmd = func() *cobra.Command { cmd := &cobra.Command{Use: "all", ...}; cmd.PersistentFlags()...; return cmd }

key_file_guides.role: Defines parent Cmd plus ListCmd, InvoiceCmd, AllCmd for gathering invoice collection.
key_file_guides.watch_for: InvoiceCmd hardcodes `Namespace: "default"` for CustomerID — a known tech debt. If multi-namespace support is needed, add a --namespace flag and thread it through the input struct. Do not replicate this pattern in new sub-commands.

anti_patterns: Hardcoding namespace strings — InvoiceCmd already has this debt; do not replicate it in new commands
Constructing billing service instances locally instead of using internal.App
Passing nil CollectionAt — always supply time.Now() or a flag-driven value
Registering multi-value filter flags as StringVar instead of StringSliceVar

decisions.decision: Use billingworkercollect input structs rather than individual parameters
decisions.rationale: Input structs evolve independently of the CLI; adding new fields to the struct does not require changing every call site in this package.

code_examples.scenario: Adding a new collect sub-command with namespace and customer filters

cmd/jobs/billing/subscriptionsync.purpose: Cobra sub-command package for subscription-to-invoice sync reconciliation (list syncable subscriptions, sync all). Delegates entirely to `internal.App.BillingSubscriptionReconciler` using typed input structs from the `reconciler` package; provides the manual recovery path for missed billing-worker Kafka events.

patterns.name: Typed reconciler input structs with embedding
patterns.description: Use `reconciler.ReconcilerListSubscriptionsInput` and embed it inside `reconciler.ReconcilerAllInput` so list and all-run filters stay structurally identical and CLI flags are reusable.
patterns.example: internal.App.BillingSubscriptionReconciler.All(cmd.Context(), reconciler.ReconcilerAllInput{ReconcilerListSubscriptionsInput: reconciler.ReconcilerListSubscriptionsInput{Namespaces: namespaces, Customers: customerIDs, Lookback: lookback}, Force: force})

patterns.name: Default lookback constant
patterns.description: Define a `defaultLookback` constant (`24 * time.Hour`) and use it as the flag default so behavior is explicit and consistent across sub-commands.

patterns.name: Force flag for idempotent re-runs
patterns.description: AllCmd exposes a `--force` (`-f`) bool flag mapped to `ReconcilerAllInput.Force` to bypass sync-state checks for manual crash recovery runs.
patterns.example: cmd.PersistentFlags().BoolVar(&force, "f", false, "force reconciliation (even if the sync state would not necessarily require it)")

patterns.name: Per-sub-command flag registration
patterns.description: Filter flags (namespaces, customerIDs, lookback) are registered on each sub-command individually via `cmd.PersistentFlags()`, not on the parent Cmd, because they are not universally needed.

patterns.name: cmd.Context() for context propagation
patterns.description: Pass `cmd.Context()` to all reconciler calls; never substitute `context.Background()`.

key_file_guides.role: Defines parent Cmd plus ListCmd and AllCmd for subscription sync. AllCmd is the primary recovery tool for missed billing-worker events.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Package-level vars for filters are shared across sub-commands; registering the same flag on both sub-commands means the last flag-parse wins during a single invocation — avoid duplicate registration of the same var on both ListCmd and AllCmd if they execute in the same process run.

anti_patterns: Calling reconciler methods without a lookback filter — results in scanning all historical subscriptions
Constructing a reconciler instance locally instead of using internal.App.BillingSubscriptionReconciler
Omitting the --force flag on AllCmd — manual recovery runs need it to bypass sync-state guards
Passing primitives directly to reconciler instead of using the typed input structs

decisions.rationale: Keeps list and all-run filter structs structurally identical so the CLI flags are reusable and the reconciler API stays DRY. Adding a new filter field to the list input automatically becomes available to the all-run command.

code_examples.scenario: Adding a new subscription-sync sub-command with namespace and customer filters using the reconciler pattern
